note: Self-contained evals — the proposal content is provided inline in each prompt (no dependency on a live GitHub issue; earlier evals referenced #204/#211, which are unrelated real issues in the upstream repo). Synthetic issue numbers (#900+) are filename labels only.

evals.prompt: Draft a maintainer review for content-proposal issue #900. The proposal is provided inline below — review it directly, no GitHub fetch needed. Proposal: UPDATE the existing risk `riskPromptInjection` to add a documented real-world example — an indirect prompt-injection attack in which instructions hidden in a retrieved web page caused an AI assistant to take an unintended action, reported by a security researcher; the author supplies the source as a plain URL. The proposal is otherwise specific and well-scoped — fit to proceed with minor corrections.
evals.expected_output: Applies scripts/agents/issue-response-reviewer.md; because the proposal is fit to proceed, it DRAFTS a Proposed YAML block for the updated risk; the source citation in that YAML uses externalReferences (type/id/title/https) referenced by a {{ref:identifier}} sentinel in the example text — never an inline URL or an <a> HTML anchor. Written to a local draft-900-comment.md; not posted.
expectations: Reads and applies scripts/agents/issue-response-reviewer.md as the source of truth rather than re-deriving the review rules
Reaches a fit-to-proceed verdict and DRAFTS a Proposed YAML block (this is the citation-path case the other evals do not exercise)
The drafted YAML cites the source via externalReferences + {{ref:identifier}} sentinels — NEVER an inline URL or an <a> HTML anchor (ADR-016/017)
Does not reference the retired relevantQuestions field or an 'Opus agent'/vendor-specific gate
Writes to a local file named draft-900-comment.md and does not attempt to post to GitHub

evals.prompt: Draft a maintainer review for content-proposal issue #901. Proposal (inline, no fetch needed): a NEW risk — 'an agent that serves multiple tenants can leak one tenant's credentials into another tenant's session or context'. Review it.
evals.expected_output: The overlap analysis catches the existing riskCrossTenantCredentialPropagation, which already covers this exact threat; verdict RETHINK (duplicate); no Proposed YAML drafted; recommends merging/extending the existing entry. Local draft-901-comment.md; not posted.
expectations: Reads and applies scripts/agents/issue-response-reviewer.md and runs the overlap analysis against risks.yaml
Identifies the existing riskCrossTenantCredentialPropagation as covering the same threat and reaches a RETHINK verdict (does not draft a new duplicate risk)
Recommends merging/extending the existing entry rather than adding a new one
Writes to a local file named draft-901-comment.md and does not post

evals.prompt: Draft a maintainer review for content-proposal issue #902. Proposal (inline, no fetch needed): a NEW persona — 'AI Red Team', responsible for adversarially testing deployed AI systems, with draft responsibilities and five identification questions supplied by the author.
evals.expected_output: Treats persona as a valid reviewable type; applies the canonical spec's persona gates (persona-model consistency and the identificationQuestions style guide — count 5-7, second-person, parenthetical examples) and surfaces the high necessity bar (is this genuinely distinct from existing personas, per ADR-021?). Local draft-902-comment.md; not posted.
expectations: Treats persona as a valid issue type and reviews it (does not claim only risk/control/component are supported)
Applies the persona-specific gates (persona-model consistency + the identificationQuestions style guide) and surfaces the necessity question vs existing personas
Uses the current identificationQuestions field, not the retired relevantQuestions
Writes to a local file named draft-902-comment.md and does not post

evals.prompt: Can you use the draft-issue-comment approach to review the YAML changes in this open PR before I merge it?
evals.expected_output: Redirects: this skill is for content-proposal issues, not PR diffs; points to content-reviewer in diff mode.
expectations: Recognizes a PR/diff review is out of scope for this skill
Redirects to content-reviewer in diff mode
Does not fabricate an issue-style review of a PR diff
